title: Task-driven onboarding workflow
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to run new hires through a task-driven onboarding workflow with mandatory stages, so that every joiner completes a consistent, complete onboarding process.
acceptance_criteria: Given a newly hired employee, when onboarding is initiated, then a task-driven workflow is created with the mandatory stages in order: Offer Acceptance, Document Submission, Document Verification, Asset Assignment, and Induction Completion.; Given the onboarding workflow, when any mandatory stage is incomplete, then the workflow cannot be marked as fully complete and the joiner is reported as still onboarding.; Given the detailed task specifications, when configuring onboarding tasks, then they follow the task checklist definitions referenced in the Task Checklist BRD (SatelliteHR-TaskChecklist-BRD.md).; Given each onboarding task, when it is created, then it is assignable, trackable, and its completion status is auditable.

title: Offer acceptance and document submission stages
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to accept my offer and submit my documents through the onboarding workflow, so that my joining formalities are captured digitally.
acceptance_criteria: Given an initiated onboarding, when I complete the Offer Acceptance stage, then the workflow advances to Document Submission.; Given the Document Submission stage, when I upload the required documents, then they are recorded against my onboarding tasks for verification.; Given a missing or mandatory document, when I attempt to complete Document Submission, then I cannot proceed until all required documents are submitted.; Given my submitted documents, when I view my onboarding, then I can see the status of each task and stage.

title: Document verification and asset assignment
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to verify submitted documents and assign assets during onboarding, so that the new hire is compliant and equipped before induction.
acceptance_criteria: Given submitted documents, when I review them in the Document Verification stage, then I can mark them verified or request corrections.; Given a request for corrections, when the employee resubmits, then the documents return for re-verification before the stage can complete.; Given verified documents, when I complete the Asset Assignment stage, then assets are recorded as assigned to the employee.; Given both Document Verification and Asset Assignment are complete, when the workflow proceeds, then Induction Completion becomes available.

title: Induction completion
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to track induction completion as the final onboarding stage, so that a new hire is formally considered fully onboarded.
acceptance_criteria: Given all prior onboarding stages are complete, when the Induction Completion stage is finished, then the onboarding workflow is marked complete.; Given an incomplete induction, when reporting on onboarding status, then the employee appears as still onboarding.; Given onboarding completion, when the workflow closes, then the completion event is recorded in the audit trail.

title: Probation confirmation decision workflow
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to evaluate an employee against probation criteria and record a confirmation decision, so that probation outcomes are handled consistently.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee on probation, when the confirmation workflow is initiated, then the configured evaluation criteria are presented for assessment.; Given a completed evaluation, when I submit a decision, then I can select exactly one outcome of Confirm, Extend, or Initiate Separation.; Given a selected outcome, when the decision is submitted, then it is routed for approval and not yet applied.

title: Probation approval hierarchy
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want probation confirmation decisions to route through the approval hierarchy of Manager, Department Head, and HR, so that outcomes are properly authorized.
acceptance_criteria: Given a probation decision, when it is submitted, then it routes for approval in the order Manager to Department Head to HR.; Given a required approver has not acted, when checking the decision status, then the decision remains pending and unapplied.; Given any approver rejects, when the decision is returned, then the outcome is not applied and the workflow reflects the rejection.; Given all approvals are granted, when the workflow completes, then the selected outcome (Confirm, Extend, or Initiate Separation) takes effect.

title: Employee transfers across department, location, and company
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to initiate inter-department, inter-location, and inter-company transfers with approval workflows, so that employee moves are governed and authorized.
acceptance_criteria: Given a transfer request, when I select the transfer type, then inter-department, inter-location, and inter-company options are supported.; Given a transfer request, when it is submitted, then it routes through the applicable approval workflow before taking effect.; Given a pending transfer, when checking status, then the employee's current assignment remains unchanged until the transfer is approved and effective.

title: Transfer effective dating
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to set an effective date for a transfer, so that the change applies at the correct point in time.
acceptance_criteria: Given an approved transfer, when I specify an effective date, then the transfer takes effect on that date.; Given a future-dated transfer, when the effective date has not been reached, then the employee's current assignment remains unchanged.; Given the effective date is reached, when the system processes the transfer, then the new assignment becomes active automatically.

title: Transfer downstream impact assessment
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a downstream impact assessment when transferring an employee, so that assets, leave balances, and policies are correctly adjusted.
acceptance_criteria: Given a transfer in progress, when the impact assessment runs, then it surfaces impacts on assets, leave balances, and policies.; Given identified impacts, when the transfer is completed, then affected assets, leave balances, and policies are reconciled to the new assignment.; Given an inter-company or inter-location transfer, when policies differ at the destination, then policy differences are flagged in the impact assessment before completion.

title: Exit request, approval, and notice period handling
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to raise an exit request that follows an approval workflow with notice period handling, so that my separation is processed correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given I want to leave, when I submit an exit request, then it routes through the exit approval workflow.; Given an approved exit, when notice period rules apply, then the notice period is captured and tracked against my exit.; Given my exit is in progress, when I view its status, then I can see approval status and notice period progress.

title: Parallel clearance and exit completion tracking
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a parallel-by-function clearance workflow with exit completion tracking, so that all departments sign off and the exit is fully closed.
acceptance_criteria: Given an approved exit, when clearance is initiated, then clearance tasks are issued in parallel across the relevant functions.; Given clearance in progress, when checking exit status, then completion is tracked per function and overall.; Given a pending functional clearance, when finalization is attempted, then the exit cannot be finalized until all functional clearances complete.; Given all functional clearances are complete, when the exit is finalized, then exit completion is recorded in the audit trail.
source: FR 6.15.4 (Exit Management — clearance workflow, exit completion tracking)

title: Configurable, auditable, and reportable lifecycle workflows
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want all lifecycle workflows to be configurable, auditable, and reportable, so that I can tailor processes, evidence compliance, and analyze outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a lifecycle workflow (onboarding, probation, transfer, exit), when I configure its stages and rules, then the changes apply to subsequent workflow instances.; Given any lifecycle action, when it is performed, then it is recorded in an audit trail with actor, timestamp, and outcome.; Given lifecycle data, when I run reports, then onboarding, probation, transfer, and exit activity is reportable.

title: Onboarding for non-user new hires
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to run the onboarding workflow on behalf of new hires who are not portal users (Employee (Non-User)), so that their joining is completed even without self-service access.
acceptance_criteria: Given a new hire who is an Employee (Non-User), when onboarding is initiated, then the same mandatory stages (Offer Acceptance, Document Submission, Document Verification, Asset Assignment, Induction Completion) are created.; Given the employee has no portal access, when Offer Acceptance and Document Submission are required, then I can record acceptance and upload documents on their behalf.; Given onboarding tasks are completed by an admin proxy, when actions are recorded, then the audit trail captures that they were performed on behalf of the non-user employee.

title: Probation extension and separation follow-through
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the Extend and Initiate Separation probation outcomes to trigger the correct follow-up, so that each decision leads to the right next lifecycle action.
acceptance_criteria: Given an approved outcome of Confirm, when it takes effect, then the employee's status changes to confirmed.; Given an approved outcome of Extend, when it takes effect, then a new probation end date and evaluation cycle are set.; Given an approved outcome of Initiate Separation, when it takes effect, then the exit management workflow is initiated for the employee.; Given any probation outcome, when it is applied, then the decision, approvals, and follow-up action are recorded in the audit trail.

title: Cross-company transfer authorization
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to authorize inter-company transfers that move an employee between companies in my group, so that cross-entity moves are governed at the group level.
acceptance_criteria: Given an inter-company transfer between two companies in the same group, when it is submitted, then it routes for group-level approval in addition to company approvals.; Given an approved inter-company transfer, when it takes effect on the effective date, then the employee is reassigned to the destination company with assets, leave balances, and policies reconciled per the impact assessment.; Given an inter-company transfer, when it is completed, then the move is auditable and reportable across both companies.

title: Functional clearance sign-off
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want each function to complete its clearance task independently during exit, so that departmental sign-offs (IT, Finance, HR, Admin) are captured before exit finalization.
acceptance_criteria: Given an initiated exit clearance, when a functional clearance task is assigned to a function owner, then that owner can complete or reject their clearance independently of other functions.; Given a rejected or pending functional clearance, when overall status is viewed, then the outstanding function and its owner are identified.; Given all functional owners have signed off, when the exit is finalized, then the aggregated clearance is recorded and the exit is closed.
source: FR 6.15.4 (Exit Management — clearance workflow, parallel by function)

title: Group-level lifecycle reporting and audit oversight
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to configure lifecycle workflows and review audit trails and reports across the companies in my group, so that I have consistent governance and oversight over lifecycle events.
acceptance_criteria: Given lifecycle workflows across my group companies, when I set configuration standards, then applicable settings can be governed consistently across those companies.; Given lifecycle activity in my group, when I run reports, then onboarding, probation, transfer, and exit activity is reportable across companies.; Given any lifecycle action in my group, when I review the audit trail, then actions are auditable with actor, timestamp, and outcome.
